assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response does not explicitly explain the atomic recreation process or distinguish it from rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'All 4 TPU v5e slices may not be maintained simultaneously, potentially causing cascading failures' which implies interconnected failure behavior

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'Your training job will be interrupted and likely terminated during maintenance'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'You might face challenges getting the same resource allocation immediately after maintenance' and notes 'The interruption duration is unpredictable (minutes to hours)'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends 'Create a final checkpoint manually before the maintenance window'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes 'Implement automatic job resumption logic in your training script' and shows example configuration for restart policies

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or deferral options

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response does not discuss maintenance windows or scheduling options to control timing
